title: Update header navigation: remove Social Media & Login links, add Sign Up dropdown with Login option

requirements.summary: Remove 'Social Media' link from Navigation component entirely (both desktop nav and mobile hamburger menu)
acceptanceCriteria: The 'Social Media' link is absent from the desktop navigation bar.
The 'Social Media' link is absent from the mobile hamburger menu.
All other existing nav links (Home, Services, About, Blog, Contact) remain intact.

file_operations.description: Remove the 'Social Media' navigation link from both the desktop navigation array and the mobile menu rendering logic. Ensure the link does not appear in either the desktop nav bar or the mobile hamburger menu, while preserving all other nav links (Home, Services, About, Blog, Contact).

requirements.summary: Remove standalone 'Login' link from Navigation component (both desktop and mobile)
acceptanceCriteria: No standalone 'Login' link exists in the desktop navigation bar.
No standalone 'Login' link exists in the mobile hamburger menu.
Removing it does not break any other navigation element.

file_operations.description: Remove the standalone 'Login' link from both the desktop navigation bar and the mobile hamburger menu. Ensure the removal does not affect other navigation elements or session-aware logic. The login option will only be accessible via the Sign Up dropdown created in REQ-57.

requirements.summary: Replace 'Sign Up' and 'Login' links with a single golden yellow 'Sign Up' primary CTA button with dropdown menu
acceptanceCriteria: A single 'Sign Up' button with golden yellow (#f5c518) background appears on the right side of the desktop nav.
Clicking or hovering the 'Sign Up' button opens a dropdown with two options: 'Sign Up' and 'Login'.
Clicking 'Sign Up' in the dropdown navigates to /customer-signup.
Clicking 'Login' in the dropdown navigates to /customer-login.
The dropdown has a dark navy background with white text and golden yellow hover highlight.
On mobile, the hamburger menu contains 'Sign Up' and 'Login' as direct tappable links.
If a customer session is active, the Sign Up dropdown is replaced by a 'My Account' link to /customer-dashboard.
The phone number remains visible in the header to the right of the nav links.
The 'Get Free Consultation' button (if present in the existing nav) is unaffected.

file_operations.description: Replace the individual 'Sign Up' and 'Login' links with a single golden yellow (#f5c518) primary CTA button labeled 'Sign Up' that opens a dropdown menu on click or hover. The dropdown should display two options: (1) 'Sign Up' (styled prominently) linking to /customer-signup, and (2) 'Login' (secondary option) linking to /customer-login. Style the dropdown with a dark navy (#0a1628) background, white text, and golden yellow hover highlight. Position this button on the right side of the desktop nav alongside the phone number. In the mobile hamburger menu, render 'Sign Up' and 'Login' as separate tappable rows without a dropdown. If a customer session is active (sessionStorage key 'ymw_customer_session' is set), hide the Sign Up dropdown button and instead show a 'My Account' link pointing to /customer-dashboard, consistent with the existing session-aware logic. Verify that the phone number and any 'Get Free Consultation' button remain unaffected.

requirements.summary: Ensure final desktop Navigation layout follows the structure: Logo (left), nav links (center), phone number and Sign Up button (right), with full responsive support
acceptanceCriteria: Logo is on the far left of the navigation bar.
Navigation links (Home, Services, About, Blog, Contact) are centered.
Phone number and Sign Up button are on the far right.
Layout is fully responsive and collapses into a hamburger menu on mobile.
No extra top-level links appear between the centered links and the right-side elements.

file_operations.description: Verify and ensure the final desktop Navigation layout follows this structure: YMW branding logo on the far left, navigation links (Home, Services, About, Blog, Contact) centered in the middle, and phone number (+91 8102906339) with the 'Sign Up' primary CTA button on the far right. Ensure no other top-level links appear between the centered nav links and the right-side elements. Confirm the layout remains fully responsive and collapses into the hamburger menu on mobile screens.
